inputs.name: disabled
inputs.description:
Whether or not an accordion item is disabled from user interaction.
When `true`, prevents the user from interacting with the item.

inputs.name: value
inputs.description: A string value for the accordion item. All items within an accordion should use a unique value.

outputs.name: onOpenChange
outputs.description: Event handler called when the panel open state changes.

inputs.name: collapsible
inputs.description:
When type is "single", allows closing content when clicking trigger for an open item.
When type is "multiple", this prop has no effect.

inputs.name: defaultValue
inputs.description:
The default active value of the item(s).

Use when you do not need to control the state of the item(s).

inputs.name: dir
inputs.description: The reading direction of the accordion when applicable. If omitted, assumes LTR (left-to-right) reading mode.

inputs.name: disabled
inputs.description: Whether the Accordion is disabled.

inputs.name: keepMounted
inputs.description:
Whether to keep the content of collapsed items mounted in the DOM.
When `true`, closed panels keep their element in the DOM instead of
receiving a `hidden` attribute. Applies to the always-mounted
`rdxAccordionContent`; the `rdxAccordionContentPresence` variant always
unmounts.

inputs.name: loopFocus
inputs.description: Whether keyboard focus wraps from the last item to the first and vice versa.

inputs.name: multiple
inputs.description:
Allow multiple panels to be open simultaneously.
Takes precedence over `type` when `true`.

inputs.name: orientation
inputs.description: The orientation of the accordion.

inputs.name: type
inputs.description: Determines whether a "single" or "multiple" items can be selected at a time.

inputs.name: value
inputs.description: The controlled value of the item to expand.

outputs.name: onValueChange
outputs.description: Event handler called when the expanded state of an item changes.

parts.description: An overlay displayed beneath the alert dialog popup.
A button that closes the alert dialog.
An accessible description for the alert dialog.

A container for the alert dialog contents. Wraps the dialog popup, which reads alert semantics
(role, modal, dismissal) from the alert root's variant.

Structural directive that teleports the alert dialog content (backdrop + popup) into a container
(default `document.body`) while the dialog is open, keeping it mounted until its CSS exit
`@keyframes` finish. It composes the structural {@link RdxDialogPortal}, inheriting the dialog
presence context.

Use the explicit `<ng-template rdxAlertDialogPortal>` form; pass `[container]` for a custom target.

Dev-mode guard: `rdxAlertDialogPortal` is now structural, so the old `<div rdxAlertDialogPortal>`
markup would silently stop portaling — fail loudly instead.

Groups all parts of the alert dialog.

Composes the Dialog primitive and forces alert-dialog semantics: it is always modal, never
dismisses on outside pointer / focus-out interactions (Escape still closes), and renders its
popup with `role="alertdialog"`. Assemble it from the `rdxAlertDialog*` parts (Popup, Backdrop,
Title, Description, Close, Portal, Viewport), which are thin wrappers around the dialog parts.
An accessible title for the alert dialog.
A button that opens the alert dialog. Behaves exactly like the dialog trigger.
A scrollable positioning container for the alert dialog popup.

Directive to maintain an aspect ratio for an element.
The element will have its `padding-bottom` dynamically calculated
based on the provided aspect ratio to maintain the desired ratio.
The content inside the element will be positioned absolutely.

inputs.name: ratio
inputs.description:
The desired aspect ratio (e.g., 16/9).
By default, it is set to 1 (which results in a square, 1:1).

parts.description:
Optional positioning anchor for the popup. Put it on the element the popup should align to — for
example a wrapper that holds the input plus an icon/clear button. When present it takes precedence
over the input. Reuses the combobox anchor behavior.

An optional arrow that points from the popup to the anchor. Composes the popper arrow directly
(the same building block the combobox arrow uses).

An overlay rendered beneath the popup in `modal` mode. Exposes `data-open` / `data-closed` for
animation. Reuses the combobox backdrop.
Clears the input value. Hidden when there is nothing to clear.

A polite, atomic live region announcing the "no results" message. Like the combobox empty part, the
element stays mounted and visible at all times (never hidden/unmounted) so the transition to empty is
announced; only its projected content is rendered conditionally.

Accessible label for a {@link RdxAutocompleteGroup}. Wires itself up via `aria-labelledby`. Reuses
the combobox group label.

Groups related options under a shared label. Hides itself when all of its items are filtered out.
Reuses the combobox group (and exposes the combobox group context for the label + items).
Decorative icon inside the trigger. Hidden from assistive technology. Reuses the combobox icon.

Optional wrapper around the input and its adornments (icon, clear, trigger). Mirrors the input's
state via `data-*` so the whole group can be styled together (focus ring, disabled, etc.).

The autocomplete text input. Holds DOM focus at all times; the highlighted option is referenced via
`aria-activedescendant`. In `both` / `inline` modes it shows the active item's inline completion
with the completed suffix selected. Integrates with Field for labeling and validation state.

inputs.name: id
inputs.description: The input id; Field labels and descriptions reference it for accessible relationships.

inputs.name: invalid
inputs.description: Marks the input as invalid independently of any Field state.

parts.description:
Renders only when its item is selected (e.g. a checkmark). Reuses the combobox item indicator,
which reads the item context provided by {@link RdxAutocompleteItem}.

A selectable suggestion. Registers itself with the root for filtering and navigation. Highlight is
virtual (`data-highlighted` + `aria-activedescendant` on the input) — items never take DOM focus.
Selecting an item writes its text into the input. Unlike the combobox item, the `value` is optional;
when omitted, selection falls back to the option's text content (autocomplete's value is the input string).

inputs.name: disabled
inputs.description: Whether the option is disabled.

inputs.name: index
inputs.description: The option's index in the list. Required in virtualized mode.

inputs.name: textValue
inputs.description: Explicit text matched against the query and written to the input. Defaults to text content.

inputs.name: value
inputs.description:
The explicit `[value]`, or `undefined` when omitted. Read the resolved {@link value} instead —
it falls back to the text content only when no value was bound (so explicit falsy values like
`0` / `''` / `null` are preserved for the filter and selection).

parts.description:
An accessible label for the autocomplete. Registers its id so the input (and trigger) reference it
via `aria-labelledby`. Reuses the combobox label behavior.

The listbox (or grid) container for suggestions. Carries the id referenced by the input's
`aria-controls`, and switches its role to `grid` when the root has `grid` enabled.

The popup surface. Composes the popper content (for `data-side` / `data-align`) and a dismissable
layer for outside-dismiss. It does **not** trap focus — focus stays in the input throughout. When
the input lives **inside** the popup (the "input in popup" / emoji-picker pattern), it moves focus
to the input once the popup is positioned.

Structural directive that teleports the autocomplete popup into a container (default
`document.body`) while the autocomplete is open, keeping it mounted until any CSS exit `@keyframes`
finishes. Composes the structural {@link RdxComboboxPortal} (which reads the open state from the
combobox root context provided by `RdxAutocompleteRoot`).

Apply it with the `*` microsyntax on the positioner —
`<div *rdxAutocompletePortal rdxAutocompletePositioner>` — or as an explicit
`<ng-template rdxAutocompletePortal>`. For a custom container use the explicit form with `[container]`.

Dev-mode guard: `rdxAutocompletePortal` is now structural, so the old `<div rdxAutocompletePortal>`
markup would silently stop portaling — fail loudly instead.

Positions the autocomplete popup relative to the input anchor using the popper engine.

A "thin" positioner (ADR 0012): it inherits the full popper positioning surface — the inputs
(`side`, `sideOffset`, `align`, …), the `placed` output, and the host bindings — from
{@link RdxPopperContentWrapper}, and only declares autocomplete's Base UI-aligned defaults through
the config provider (the same building block the combobox positioner uses).

inputs.name: align
inputs.description: How to align the popup relative to the specified side. May change when collisions occur.

inputs.name: alignOffset
inputs.description: An offset in pixels from the `start` or `end` alignment options.

inputs.name: anchor
inputs.description: An element to position the popup against. Defaults to the popper anchor.

inputs.name: arrowPadding
inputs.description:
Minimum distance to maintain between the arrow and the edges of the popup.
If your content has border-radius, this will prevent it from overflowing the corners.

inputs.name: avoidCollisions
inputs.description: When `true`, overrides the `side` and `align` preferences to prevent collisions with boundary edges.

inputs.name: collisionBoundary
inputs.description:
The element used as the collision boundary.
By default this is the viewport, though you can provide additional element(s) to be included in this check.

inputs.name: collisionPadding
inputs.description:
The distance in pixels from the boundary edges where collision detection should occur.
Accepts a number (same for all sides), or a partial padding object, for example: `{ top: 20, left: 20 }`.

inputs.name: hideWhenDetached
inputs.description: Whether to hide the content when the trigger becomes fully occluded.

inputs.name: positionStrategy
inputs.description: The type of CSS position property to use.

inputs.name: side
inputs.description:
The preferred side of the anchor to render against when open.
Will be reversed when collisions occur and avoidCollisions is enabled.

inputs.name: sideOffset
inputs.description: Distance between the anchor and the popup in pixels.

inputs.name: sticky
inputs.description:
The sticky behavior on the align axis. `partial` will keep the
content in the boundary as long as the trigger is at least partially
in the boundary whilst "always" will keep the content in the boundary
regardless.

inputs.name: updatePositionStrategy
inputs.description: Strategy to update the position of the floating element on every animation frame.

outputs.name: placed
outputs.description: Emits when the element is placed.

parts.description:
Root of an Autocomplete — a text input with a filtered list of suggestions. A thin configuration over
the shared combobox engine (ADR 0014) with `selectionMode: 'none'`, so its value **is** the input
string: typing, selecting an item, or clearing all change a single string value. Implements
`ControlValueAccessor` (the form value is the input string).

inputs.name: autoHighlight
defaultOpen
inputs.description: Initial open state when uncontrolled.

inputs.name: defaultValue
inputs.description: Initial input text when uncontrolled.

inputs.name: dir
inputs.description: Text direction.

inputs.name: disabled
inputs.description: Whether the autocomplete is disabled.

inputs.name: filter
inputs.description:
Filter applied to items against the input query (only when {@link mode} is `'list'` / `'both'`).
`undefined` → locale-aware contains; a function → custom `(value, query, itemToString)`; `null` →
built-in filtering disabled.

inputs.name: grid
inputs.description: Whether the list is laid out as a 2D grid (enables row/column arrow navigation).

inputs.name: highlightItemOnHover
inputs.description: Whether moving the pointer over an item highlights it.

inputs.name: isItemEqualToValue
inputs.description: How item values are compared for equality (a comparator function or an object key). Base UI prop name.

inputs.name: items
inputs.description: The full set of item values for {@link virtualized} mode.

inputs.name: itemToStringValue
inputs.description: Converts an item value to its string label (filter text + input text on selection).

inputs.name: keepHighlight
inputs.description: Whether a pointer-driven highlight is kept when the cursor leaves the list.

inputs.name: limit
inputs.description: Maximum number of matching items to show. `-1` (default) means no limit.

inputs.name: loopFocus
inputs.description: Whether keyboard navigation wraps at the list boundaries.

inputs.name: modal
inputs.description: Whether the popup is modal: locks page scroll and makes outside content inert while open.

inputs.name: mode
inputs.description: Filtering / inline-completion behavior. See {@link AutocompleteMode}.

inputs.name: open
inputs.description: Whether the popup is open.

inputs.name: openOnInputClick
inputs.description: Whether clicking the input opens the popup. Defaults to `false` (Base UI default).

inputs.name: readOnly
inputs.description: Whether the autocomplete is read-only.

inputs.name: required
inputs.description: Whether a value is required (for forms).

inputs.name: submitOnItemClick
inputs.description: Whether selecting an item requests submit of the closest form.

inputs.name: value
inputs.description: The input text. This is the form value (controlled / uncontrolled via {@link defaultValue}).

inputs.name: virtualized
inputs.description: Whether the list is externally virtualized (navigation runs over {@link items} by index).

outputs.name: onItemHighlighted
outputs.description: Emits as the highlight moves, with the item's value, its index in {@link filteredItems}, and the reason.

outputs.name: onOpenChange
outputs.description: Emits when the popup opens or closes.

outputs.name: onOpenChangeComplete
outputs.description: Emits after the open/close transition (including any exit animation) finishes.

outputs.name: onValueChange
outputs.description: Emits when the input value changes (typing, selection, or clear), with the reason.

parts.description:
A row in a grid-layout autocomplete list. Groups the items in one row so the root can navigate by
row (ArrowUp / ArrowDown) and within a row (ArrowLeft / ArrowRight). Only meaningful when the root
has `grid` enabled; the root resolves an item's row from its nearest `[rdxAutocompleteRow]` ancestor.
A visual separator between groups of suggestions (`role="separator"`). Reuses the combobox separator.

A polite live region for async status (loading, result counts) announced without moving focus.
Reuses the combobox status region.

Toggles the autocomplete popup. Reuses the combobox trigger: a `tabindex="-1"` toggle when the input
sits outside the popup, or the focusable `role="combobox"` control when the input is inside it.

Renders the current input value as text. Useful for a read-only display of the committed value. Read `slotText()` in the template. Exposes `data-placeholder` while the value is empty. ```html {{ value.slotText() }} ```

inputs.name: placeholder
inputs.description: Text shown when the value is empty.

inputs.name: delayMs
inputs.description: Useful for delaying rendering so it only appears for those with slower connections.

outputs.name: onLoadingStatusChange
outputs.description:
A callback providing information about the loading status of the image.
This is useful in case you want to control more precisely what to render as the image is loading.

parts.description:
Headless button behavior, modeled on Base UI's `useButton`.

Renders accessible button semantics on a native `<button>` or on any other
element (e.g. `<a>`, `<span>`). Carries no styles — state is exposed via
`data-disabled` for consumers to style.

inputs.name: disabled
inputs.description: Whether the button is disabled.

inputs.name: focusableWhenDisabled
inputs.description: Keep the button focusable while disabled (uses `aria-disabled`).

inputs.name: type
inputs.description: The `type` attribute applied to native `<button>` hosts.

inputs.name: day
inputs.description: The date value provided to the cell trigger

inputs.name: month
inputs.description: The month in which the cell is rendered

inputs.name: date
inputs.description: The date of the cell

inputs.name: nextPage
inputs.description: The function to be used for the `next page`. Overwrites the nextPage function set on the `CalendarRoot`.

inputs.name: prevPage
inputs.description: The function to be used for the `prev page`. Overwrites the prevPage function set on the `CalendarRoot`.

inputs.name: calendarLabel
inputs.description: The accessible label for the calendar

inputs.name: defaultPlaceholder
inputs.description: The default placeholder date

inputs.name: dir
inputs.description: The reading direction of the calendar when applicable.

inputs.name: disabled
inputs.description: Whether the calendar is disabled

inputs.name: fixedWeeks
inputs.description: Whether to always display 6 weeks in the calendar

inputs.name: isDateDisabled
inputs.description: A function that returns whether a date is disabled

inputs.name: isDateUnavailable
inputs.description: A function that returns whether a date is unavailable

inputs.name: maxValue
inputs.description: The maximum date that can be selected

inputs.name: minValue
inputs.description: The minimum date that can be selected

inputs.name: numberOfMonths
inputs.description: The number of months to display at once

inputs.name: pagedNavigation
inputs.description: This property causes the previous and next buttons to navigate by the number of months displayed at once, rather than one month

inputs.name: placeholder
inputs.description:
The placeholder date, which is used to determine what month to display when no date is selected.
This updates as the user navigates the calendar and can be used to programmatically control the calendar view

inputs.name: preventDeselect
inputs.description: Whether to prevent the user from deselecting a date without selecting another date first

inputs.name: readonly
inputs.description: Whether the calendar is readonly

inputs.name: value
inputs.description: The controlled checked state of the calendar

inputs.name: weekdayFormat
inputs.description: The format to use for the weekday strings provided via the weekdays slot prop

inputs.name: weekDays
inputs.description: The days of the week

inputs.name: weekStartsOn
inputs.description: The day of the week to start the calendar on

parts.description:
Directive: rdxCheckboxButton
Purpose: Turns a native <button> into an accessible checkbox control bound to the Radix Checkbox context.
It mirrors the checkbox state via ARIA/data attributes, toggles on click, and prevents Enter activation per WAI-ARIA.
In forms, it stops the button's click from bubbling so only the hidden input emits the native event used for form/validator integration.

Groups a set of checkboxes that share a single array value (the names of the checked boxes).

Each child `rdxCheckboxRoot` participates by its `name`. A child marked `parent` becomes a
"select all" checkbox whose state is derived from `allValues`.

inputs.name: allValues
inputs.description: All checkbox names in the group. Required for a `parent` (select-all) checkbox.

inputs.name: defaultValue
inputs.description: The names checked initially when the group is uncontrolled.

inputs.name: disabled
inputs.description: Whether the whole group is disabled.

inputs.name: value
inputs.description: The names of the currently checked checkboxes. Use with `onValueChange` or `[(value)]`.

outputs.name: onValueChange
outputs.description: Emits the new array of checked names whenever the value changes.

inputs.name: checked
inputs.description:
The controlled checked state of the checkbox. Must be used in conjunction with onCheckedChange.

Mixed state is no longer expressed through `checked` — use the separate
`indeterminate` input (Base UI shape). This `boolean` model is what
`RdxFormCheckboxControl` / Angular Signal Forms bind to.

inputs.name: disabled
inputs.description: Whether or not the checkbox button is disabled. This prevents the user from interacting with it.

inputs.name: form
inputs.description: Associates the control with a form element.

inputs.name: indeterminate
inputs.description:
Whether the checkbox is in a mixed state: neither ticked nor unticked.
Orthogonal to `checked` and not part of the submitted form value. A user
click resolves the checkbox to `checked` and clears `indeterminate`.

inputs.name: name
inputs.description:
Name of the form control. Submitted with the form as part of a name/value pair. Inside a
`rdxCheckboxGroup` this also identifies the checkbox in the group's value array.

inputs.name: parent
inputs.description:
When inside a `rdxCheckboxGroup`, marks this as the "select all" checkbox: its state is
derived from the group's `allValues`, and toggling it checks or unchecks every child.

inputs.name: readonly
inputs.description: Whether the user should be unable to tick or untick the checkbox.

inputs.name: required
inputs.description: Whether or not the checkbox is required.

inputs.name: value
inputs.description:
The value of the checkbox. This is what is submitted with the form when the checkbox is checked.

Bound publicly as `[value]`; the TS member is named `submitValue` so the
directive can satisfy `RdxFormCheckboxControl`, whose contract reserves a
`value` member for `RdxFormValueControl` and forbids it on checkbox-style
controls. (Checkbox is not yet marked `implements` — its `checked` is still
`CheckedState`; see the `indeterminate` half of collision #1.)

parts.description: A panel with the collapsible contents.

inputs.name: hiddenUntilFound
inputs.description:
Allows the browser's built-in page search to find and expand the panel contents.
When `true`, the closed panel uses `hidden="until-found"` instead of plain `hidden`.

inputs.name: keepMounted
inputs.description:
Whether to keep the element in the DOM while the panel is closed.
When `true`, the closed panel keeps its element (no `hidden` attribute) so the consumer's
`data-closed` CSS is responsible for visually collapsing it.

parts.description: Groups all parts of the collapsible.

inputs.name: defaultOpen
inputs.description:
The open state of the collapsible when it is initially rendered.
Use when you do not need to control its open state.

inputs.name: disabled
inputs.description: Whether the component should ignore user interaction.

inputs.name: open
inputs.description:
The controlled open state of the collapsible.
`true` - expanded, `false` - collapsed.

inputs.name: panelId
inputs.description: Stable id linking the trigger's `aria-controls` to the panel.

outputs.name: onOpenChange
outputs.description: Event handler called when the open state of the collapsible changes.

outputs.name: onOpenChangeComplete
outputs.description: Event handler called after the open/close transition has finished.

parts.description: A button that opens and closes the collapsible panel.

Marks an element as a member of a collection. Items are discovered by the
{@link RdxCollectionProvider} via `contentChildren`, so registration is automatic — no manual
book-keeping or marker attributes are needed.

inputs.name: disabled
inputs.description: Whether the item is disabled. Disabled items are excluded from {@link RdxCollectionProvider.enabledItems}.

inputs.name: value
inputs.description: Arbitrary data associated with the item.

parts.description:
Collects {@link RdxCollectionItem} descendants in DOM order, reactively, using Angular's
`contentChildren`. Matches host directives too, so items composed via `hostDirectives` are found.

Optional positioning anchor for the popup. Put it on the element the popup should align to — for
example a control that wraps chips + input in `multiple` mode. When present it takes precedence
over the input (the popper resolves the first `RdxPopperAnchor` in DOM order, and this part sits
above the input). When absent, the input itself is the anchor, which is ideal when the input fills
the control.

An optional arrow that points from the popup to the anchor. Composes the popper arrow, which keeps
it aligned as the popup flips sides.

An overlay rendered beneath the popup in `modal` mode. Place it inside the portal/presence; style
it `position: fixed; inset: 0`. Give it `pointer-events: auto` so a click on it dismisses the popup
(with `modal`, the rest of the page is inert). Exposes `data-open` / `data-closed` for animation.
Removes its chip's value from the selection, keeping focus in the input.

A single selected-value chip. Provide its `value` so {@link RdxComboboxChipRemove} can deselect it.
Chips are focusable and navigable with the arrow keys; Backspace / Delete removes the chip.

inputs.name: value
inputs.description: The value this chip represents.

parts.description:
Container for the selected-value chips in `multiple` mode. Sits before the input and coordinates
arrow-key navigation across the chips (the chips themselves handle the key events). Uses
`role="toolbar"` (Base UI): it keeps NVDA in focus mode while arrow-navigating the chips, where a
plain `list` would drop into browse mode.
Clears the current selection and the input text. Hidden when there is nothing to clear.

inputs.name: disabled
inputs.description: Disables just this clear button (in addition to the combobox's own disabled / read-only state).

parts.description:
A polite, atomic live region announcing the "no results" message. Mirrors Base UI: the element
stays **mounted and visible at all times** so screen readers reliably announce the transition to
empty — only its *content* is rendered conditionally (projected when nothing matches, removed
otherwise). It must never be hidden/unmounted (`hidden`, `display:none`, `aria-hidden`, `@if`):
pulling the region out of the accessibility tree at the same instant its text appears is exactly
what suppresses the announcement.
Accessible label for a {@link RdxComboboxGroup}. Wires itself up via `aria-labelledby`.

Groups related options under a shared label. Hides itself when all of its items are filtered out,
so a group heading never lingers above an empty section.
Decorative icon inside the trigger. Hidden from assistive technology.

Optional wrapper around the input and its adornments (icon, clear, trigger). Mirrors the combobox
state via `data-*` so the whole group can be styled together (focus ring, disabled, etc.).

The combobox text input. Holds DOM focus at all times; the highlighted option is referenced via
`aria-activedescendant`. Integrates with Field for labeling, description, and validation state.

inputs.name: id
inputs.description: The input id; Field labels and descriptions reference it for accessible relationships.

inputs.name: invalid
inputs.description: Marks the input as invalid independently of any Field state.

parts.description: Renders only when its item is selected (e.g. a checkmark).

A selectable option. Registers itself with the root for filtering and navigation. Highlight is
virtual (`data-highlighted` + `aria-activedescendant` on the input) — items never take DOM focus.

inputs.name: disabled
inputs.description: Whether the option is disabled.

inputs.name: index
inputs.description:
The option's index in the list. Required in virtualized mode — it drives the deterministic id
(for `aria-activedescendant`), highlight matching, and selection when only a window is mounted.

inputs.name: textValue
inputs.description: Explicit text matched against the query. Defaults to the element's text content.

inputs.name: value
inputs.description: The option's value.

parts.description:
An accessible label for the combobox. Registers its id so the input (and trigger) reference it via
`aria-labelledby`.

The listbox container for options. Carries the id referenced by the input's `aria-controls`, exposes
`data-empty` while no options match the current query (Base UI's `ComboboxList` empty state), and
switches its `role` to `grid` when the root has `grid` enabled.

The popup surface. Composes the popper content (for `data-side` / `data-align`) and a dismissable
layer for outside-dismiss. It does **not** trap focus — focus stays in the input throughout.

Structural directive that teleports the combobox popup into a container (default `document.body`)
while the combobox is open, and keeps it mounted until any CSS exit `@keyframes` finishes.

Apply it with the `*` microsyntax on the positioner —
`<div *rdxComboboxPortal rdxComboboxPositioner>` — or as an explicit `<ng-template rdxComboboxPortal>`.
For a custom container use the explicit form with `[container]`.

inputs.name: container
inputs.description:
Optional container to portal the content into. Defaults to `document.body`. Declared here (and
forwarded to the composed {@link RdxPortalPresence}) so the autocomplete portal can re-expose it
through its own `hostDirectives`.

parts.description:
Dev-mode guard: `rdxComboboxPortal` used to be an attribute directive on a `<div>`. It is now
structural, so the old `<div rdxComboboxPortal>` markup would silently stop portaling — fail loudly
instead.

Positions the combobox popup relative to the input anchor using the popper engine.

A "thin" positioner (ADR 0012): it inherits the full popper positioning surface — the inputs
(`side`, `sideOffset`, `align`, …), the `placed` output, and the host bindings — from
{@link RdxPopperContentWrapper}, and only declares combobox's Base UI-aligned defaults through the
config provider. `provideRdxPopperContentWrapper` re-wires the `useExisting` alias + context that
the popup and arrow resolve (Angular does not inherit a base directive's `providers`).

inputs.name: align
inputs.description: How to align the popup relative to the specified side. May change when collisions occur.

inputs.name: alignOffset
inputs.description: An offset in pixels from the `start` or `end` alignment options.

inputs.name: anchor
inputs.description: An element to position the popup against. Defaults to the popper anchor.

inputs.name: arrowPadding
inputs.description:
Minimum distance to maintain between the arrow and the edges of the popup.
If your content has border-radius, this will prevent it from overflowing the corners.

inputs.name: avoidCollisions
inputs.description: When `true`, overrides the `side` and `align` preferences to prevent collisions with boundary edges.

inputs.name: collisionBoundary
inputs.description:
The element used as the collision boundary.
By default this is the viewport, though you can provide additional element(s) to be included in this check.

inputs.name: collisionPadding
inputs.description:
The distance in pixels from the boundary edges where collision detection should occur.
Accepts a number (same for all sides), or a partial padding object, for example: `{ top: 20, left: 20 }`.

inputs.name: hideWhenDetached
inputs.description: Whether to hide the content when the trigger becomes fully occluded.

inputs.name: positionStrategy
inputs.description: The type of CSS position property to use.

inputs.name: side
inputs.description:
The preferred side of the anchor to render against when open.
Will be reversed when collisions occur and avoidCollisions is enabled.

inputs.name: sideOffset
inputs.description: Distance between the anchor and the popup in pixels.

inputs.name: sticky
inputs.description:
The sticky behavior on the align axis. `partial` will keep the
content in the boundary as long as the trigger is at least partially
in the boundary whilst "always" will keep the content in the boundary
regardless.

parts.description:
A row in a grid-layout combobox list. Groups the items in one row so the root can navigate by row
(`ArrowUp` / `ArrowDown`) and within a row (`ArrowLeft` / `ArrowRight`). Only meaningful when the
root has `grid` enabled; the root resolves an item's row from its nearest `[rdxComboboxRow]` ancestor.

A visual separator between groups of options. Carries `role="separator"` with a horizontal
orientation (it divides rows in a vertical list), so assistive tech announces the grouping break.
A polite live region for async status (loading, result counts) announced without moving focus.

Toggles the combobox popup. Its semantics depend on the layout (Base UI's `inputInsidePopup`):

- **Input outside the popup** (default): a `tabindex="-1"` toggle button (`aria-haspopup="listbox"`)
  that never steals focus from the input — `Tab` lands directly on the input.
- **Input inside the popup** (e.g. a command palette / emoji picker): the trigger becomes the primary
  `role="combobox"` control — `tabindex="0"` (reachable via `Tab`), `aria-haspopup="dialog"`, and
  `ArrowDown`/`ArrowUp` open the popup (which moves focus to the inner input and highlights an item).

The trigger stays `Tab`-reachable by default and is demoted to `tabindex="-1"` only once an input is
detected *outside* the popup — so a trigger whose input lives in a not-yet-opened popup is focusable
from the first render (`inputLayout` is `unknown` until that input mounts).

Renders the current selection's label(s) — typically inside a {@link RdxComboboxTrigger} for a select-like control. Read `slotText()` in the template, or compose your own from `selectedLabels()`. Exposes `data-placeholder` while nothing is selected. ```html {{ value.slotText() }} ```

parts.description:
Registers a {@link RdxFloatingNode} into the shared {@link RdxFloatingTree} for its DI subtree and
propagates the registration handle to descendants — the reusable Angular counterpart of mounting a
Base UI `<FloatingNode>` (ADR 0015 §1, Phase 1). It is the **single** place that runs the handle
pattern; the dismissal capability (ADR 0015) and the focus manager (ADR 0017) **consume** the node /
context / tree it registers, they do not re-implement registration.

**What it owns vs. what it reads.** It provides its own {@link RdxFloatingRegistrationContext} (so
descendants resolve it with `skipSelf`) and registers/unregisters a node reactively. It does **not**
create the tree or the root context — a coordination-boundary primitive root supplies those
(`provideFloatingTree()` inherit-or-create + `provideFloatingRootContext()`); this directive injects
them. With **no** enclosing tree it runs **node-optional** (`status() === 'detached'`, `node() ===
null`), reading its context directly — the standalone `rdxDismissableLayer` case.

**Resolution (per {@link RdxFloatingParentOverride}).** Only an `inherit` node depends on the DI
parent, so only it waits on a `pending` parent; `root` / `node` overrides are independent and register
immediately. The node carries the injected {@link RDX_FLOATING_ROOT_CONTEXT} (or `null` for a
contextless intermediate). All teardown (re-resolution **and** destroy) unregisters the node and
reverts the handle.

parts.description:
An overlay displayed beneath the dialog popup.

Decorative-only, so it carries `role="presentation"` (Base UI `DialogBackdrop`). By default a **nested**
dialog renders no backdrop — the parent's already dims the page; stacking a second one double-darkens
and intercepts the parent's outside-press. Set `forceRender` to opt back in.

inputs.name: forceRender
inputs.description: Render the backdrop even for a nested dialog (off by default, matching Base UI).

parts.description: A button that closes the dialog.
An accessible description for the dialog.

A container for the dialog contents.

**ADR 0015/0017 Phase-4 migration — Dialog is the PILOT cutover onto the new floating dismissal +
focus engine. Browser-verified** by `apps/visual-regression/tests/dialog.behavior.spec.ts` (trap,
initial / return focus, Escape / outside-press / focus-out dismissal, nested-Escape deepest-first,
backdrop-not-marked).

**Mapping (legacy → new):**
- `RdxDismissableLayer` (legacy) → `RdxFloatingNodeRegistration` (registers the tree node) +
  `RdxDismissableCapability` (Escape / outside-press; reads the root context + node).
- `RdxFocusScope` (direct) → `RdxFloatingFocusManager` (composes the reworked focus scope; trap +
  markOthers + close-on-focus-out), driven by `provideFloatingFocusManagerConfig`.
- `disableOutsidePointerEvents` → the focus manager's `inert` pass marks outside elements
  non-interactive for a modal (finding #4), scoped to siblings of the popup's ancestor chain instead
  of a global `body { pointer-events: none }` lock — so the popup needs no `pointer-events: auto`.
- focus-out close moved from the dismissal capability (`focusOutside: () => false`) to the manager
  (`manager.focusOut`), per ADR 0017 §3.
- `isEventOnTrigger` preventDefault → removed: the trigger is in `context.triggers`, so the engine
  treats a press/focus on it as **inside** (no close-then-reopen).

**Remaining open items (not blockers; tracked for the full cutover):**
1. `enabled: isOpen()` releases the trap at close-start vs legacy holding it until unmount — verified
   OK (return-focus + exit-animation tests pass), but the manager's single `enabled` can't yet split
   trap(mounted) from marker/focus-out(open).
2. `markOthers` aria-hidden applies for `'trap-focus'` too (manager modal) while `aria-modal` is set
   only for `modal === true` — decide whether to split (AT review).
3. `returnFocus` orchestration is deferred → the reworked focus scope's default return-focus is used.
4. Atomic-cutover caveat: Dialog is on the new engine while other primitives are legacy — cross-primitive
   nesting (e.g. a legacy Popover inside this Dialog) is **out of scope** until the full Phase-4 cutover.

parts.description:
`RdxFloatingFocusManager` (ADR 0017 Phase 1b skeleton) — the Angular counterpart of Base UI's
`FloatingFocusManager`. It is a **coordinator** that composes three low-level focus parts (it never
inherits them, which would re-fuse trap + popup policy): the **reworked {@link RdxFocusScope}** (the
trap, via `hostDirectives`), the portal-focus bridge, and owner-`Document` guards. Per ADR 0017 §1/§2
its policies are **independent**, none derived from `modal`.

**This skeleton wires the composition + lifecycle gates:**
- `enabled` — the manager's active-ness (`mounted && !hover-open`). When off, **no trap** (and, later,
  no aria-hidden / no marker — Phase 2).
- `modal` → `RdxFocusScope.trapped`: the effective trap is `enabled() && modal()`, pushed into the
  composed focus scope through its config token (the composition seam).
- `loop` is forwarded to `RdxFocusScope`.
- `initialFocus` / `returnFocus` are **typed** here (the §2 policy contract, incl. the interaction-type
  callback forms); their full orchestration + the portal-bridge wiring + close-on-focus-out land in
  later phases.

Whether a **non-modal** popup closes when focus leaves to an unrelated node (Base UI
`closeOnFocusOut`, default `true`; Dialog sets it to `!disablePointerDismissal`). Modal popups
never close on focus-out (the trap keeps focus in).

inputs.description: Manager active-ness (ADR 0017 §2): the popup is mounted **and** not hover-opened.

inputs.name: initialFocus
inputs.description: Where focus goes when the popup opens (ADR 0017 §2).

inputs.name: modal
inputs.description: Modal popup → focus trap. Combined with `enabled` to drive the composed `RdxFocusScope`.

inputs.name: returnFocus
inputs.description: Where focus returns when the popup closes (ADR 0017 §2).

Emitted when focus leaves a non-modal popup to a node **unrelated** to the floating tree (ADR 0017
§3) — the consumer should close the popup. This is the focus-manager's focus-out close (it reads
the shared tree), replacing the dismissal capability's focus-out at the ADR 0015 Phase-4 cutover.

parts.description:
Structural directive that teleports the menu popup into a container (default `document.body`) while
the menu is open, and keeps it mounted until any CSS exit `@keyframes` finishes.

This replaces the consumer-owned `@if (root.open())` mount: it adds both teleporting *and*
exit-animation support. Apply it with the `*` microsyntax on the positioner —
`<div *rdxMenuPortal rdxMenuPositioner>` — or as an explicit `<ng-template rdxMenuPortal>`. For a
custom container, or a backdrop alongside the positioner (multi-root), use the explicit form.

Dev-mode guard: `rdxMenuPortal` is a structural directive. The old `<div rdxMenuPortal>` markup
would silently stop portaling — fail loudly instead.

Positions the menu against its trigger.

A "thin" positioner (ADR 0012): it inherits the popper positioning surface (inputs, `placed`
output, unified vars + placement attrs) from {@link RdxPopperContentWrapper} and adds the menu
defaults, the open/closed state attributes, and the deprecated `--radix-menu-*` aliases.

inputs.name: collisionBoundary
inputs.description:

inputs.name: collisionPadding
inputs.description:

Accepts a number (same for all sides), or a partial padding object, for example: `{ top: 20, left: 20 }`.

inputs.description: The type of CSS position property to use.

**ADR 0015/0017 Phase-4 migration** onto the new floating dismissal + focus engine (same pattern as
Dialog; browser-verified via `popover.behavior` Playwright). Popover-specific:
- **Hover-open disables the manager** (`enabled = isOpen && !isHoverActive`) — Base UI parity
  (`disabled={!mounted || openReason === triggerHover}`); a hover-opened popover does not trap / mark.
  (The legacy only suppressed auto-focus while still trapping — that Radix divergence is dropped.)
- Trap = `'trap-focus' || (modal === true && hasPopupClose())`; scroll / body-pointer lock + the
  popup's `pointer-events: auto` key off the full modal (`modal === true`).
- No `disablePointerDismissal` — outside-press + focus-out always close.

Note: a positioned popover does **not** auto-focus into the popup on open (pre-existing — the legacy
behaved the same; verified). The trap holds focus once it is inside. Auto-focus-on-open + redirecting a
Tab from the trigger into the popup needs the deferred portal-focus bridge / guards (ADR 0017 §6a).

structural, so the old `<div rdxSelectPortal>` markup would silently stop portaling — fail loudly
instead.

provider. It also satisfies {@link RdxPositionerImpl} (via the inherited `placed`) so the popup can
resolve it through {@link RDX_SELECT_POSITIONER_TOKEN}, the same as the item-aligned positioner.

so descendant viewports and components share one queue. `limit` caps simultaneously visible toasts.

parts.description:
A single toast — the Angular counterpart of `<Toast.Root>`. Bind the toast model from the
viewport's `@for`; this directive owns the announcement `role`, the `data-state` enter/leave
contract, swipe-to-dismiss, and the stacking variables consumers style against.

Stacking / styling contract written to the host (no transform is applied for you):
- `--toast-index` — position from the front (`0` = frontmost).
- `--toast-height` — this toast's measured height (px).
- `--toast-offset-y` — combined height (px) of the toasts stacked in front, for expanded layout.
- `--toast-swipe-movement-x` / `--toast-swipe-movement-y` — live swipe offset (px).
- `[data-front]` — present on the frontmost toast.
- `[data-expanded]` — present while the viewport is hovered/focused.
- `[data-state]` — `open` while visible, `closed` once dismissal begins.
- `[data-swiping]` / `[data-swipe-direction]` / `[data-swipe-dismiss]` — gesture state.

When the leave animation (driven by `data-state="closed"`) ends, the toast leaves the queue.

it expands the stack (`data-expanded` on each root) and pauses every auto-dismiss timer, resuming
once neither hover nor focus remains — matching Base UI.

Headless: it carries no positioning styles. Consumers position it (e.g. fixed bottom-right) and
iterate `viewport.toasts()` with `@for`.

The tooltip popup. Renders the content with `role="tooltip"`.

Structural directive that teleports the tooltip content into a container (default `document.body`)
while the tooltip is open, and keeps it mounted until any CSS exit `@keyframes` finishes.

Apply it with the `*` microsyntax on the positioner — `<div *rdxTooltipPortal rdxTooltipPositioner>`
— or as an explicit `<ng-template rdxTooltipPortal>`. For a custom container use the explicit form
with `[container]`.

Dev-mode guard: `rdxTooltipPortal` used to be an attribute directive on a `<div>`. It is now
structural, so the old `<div rdxTooltipPortal>` markup would silently stop portaling — fail loudly
instead.

Positions the tooltip popup against its trigger (or a custom anchor).

A "thin" positioner (ADR 0012): it inherits the popper positioning surface (inputs, `placed`
output, unified vars + placement attrs) from {@link RdxPopperContentWrapper} and adds tooltip's own
concerns — Base UI-aligned defaults (`side: 'top'`) via the config provider, dismiss handling
(ADR 0015 — inline {@link RdxDismissableCapability} on the shared floating tree, dismissal-only with
no focus manager), the cursor-follow pointer-through behavior (via the inherited `nonInteractive`
signal), the open/closed state attributes, and the hover grace area.

inputs.name: align
inputs.description: How to align the popup relative to the specified side. May change when collisions occur.

inputs.name: alignOffset
inputs.description: An offset in pixels from the `start` or `end` alignment options.

inputs.name: anchor
inputs.description: An element to position the popup against. Defaults to the popper anchor.

inputs.name: arrowPadding
inputs.description:
Minimum distance to maintain between the arrow and the edges of the popup.
If your content has border-radius, this will prevent it from overflowing the corners.

inputs.name: avoidCollisions
inputs.description: When `true`, overrides the `side` and `align` preferences to prevent collisions with boundary edges.

inputs.name: collisionBoundary
inputs.description:
The element used as the collision boundary.
By default this is the viewport, though you can provide additional element(s) to be included in this check.

inputs.name: collisionPadding
inputs.description:
The distance in pixels from the boundary edges where collision detection should occur.
Accepts a number (same for all sides), or a partial padding object, for example: `{ top: 20, left: 20 }`.

inputs.name: hideWhenDetached
inputs.description: Whether to hide the content when the trigger becomes fully occluded.

inputs.name: positionStrategy
inputs.description: The type of CSS position property to use.

inputs.name: side
inputs.description:
The preferred side of the anchor to render against when open.
Will be reversed when collisions occur and avoidCollisions is enabled.

inputs.name: sideOffset
inputs.description: Distance between the anchor and the popup in pixels.

inputs.name: sticky
inputs.description:
The sticky behavior on the align axis. `partial` will keep the
content in the boundary as long as the trigger is at least partially
in the boundary whilst "always" will keep the content in the boundary
regardless.

inputs.name: updatePositionStrategy
inputs.description: Strategy to update the position of the floating element on every animation frame.

outputs.name: escapeKeyDown
outputs.description: Event handler called when the escape key is down. Can be prevented.

outputs.name: pointerDownOutside
outputs.description: Event handler called when a `pointerdown` event happens outside of the popup. Can be prevented.

outputs.name: placed
outputs.description: Emits when the element is placed.

parts.description:
Mirrors a control's value into a visually hidden `<input>` so it participates in form
submission while staying out of the visual and (when `fully-hidden`) accessibility tree.

Apply it to a real `<input>` element. It carries a **single scalar value** — the composed
{@link RdxVisuallyHiddenInputBubbleDirective} owns `name`/`value`/`checked` and keeps the
native value in sync, re-dispatching `input`/`change` events so the value bubbles to the form.

A single `<input>` can only represent one form field, so object/array values are not
supported here; render one directive per field instead.

```html
<input rdxVisuallyHiddenInput [name]="'agree'" [value]="checked() ? 'on' : 'off'" />
```
